2606.06337.title: TokenMizer: Graph-Structured Session Memory for Long-Horizon LLM Context Management
2606.06337.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) deployments for long-horizon tasks face a fundamental constraint: context windows are finite while productive work sessions are not. When history exceeds the Maximum Effective Context Window (MECW), critical structured information - architectural decisions, task transitions, file histories - is silently discarded. Existing mitigations treat history as flat text, destroying the relational structure that makes sessions resumable. We present TokenMizer, an open-source proxy system that models LLM session history as a typed knowledge graph. The schema defines 14 node types and 7 edge types. A hybrid extraction pipeline populates the graph incrementally, while a three-tier checkpoint system serializes it into compact resume blocks. An 8-layer compression pipeline reduces context overhead, and a semantic cache reduces repeated-query latency. Evaluated on a controlled benchmark of 21 sessions spanning 5 domains, TokenMizer demonstrates significant token economy. It produces resume blocks averaging 78 tokens (range: 42-124) - 2x smaller than evaluated baselines (159-170 tokens) - while achieving higher decision recall (+9-17 percentage points). Crucially, baselines only preserve that a technology was mentioned; TokenMizer preserves the rationale. Across all sessions, TokenMizer achieves mean task recall 51.0%, decision recall 46.6%, and file recall 58.7%. Variance reflects domain heterogeneity: explicit imperative phrasing (software engineering) scores higher than implicit reasoning (research). Ablation studies show fuzzy label matching is the dominant improvement factor (+33 pp task recall). The heuristic compression achieves 47.3% token reduction with zero external dependencies. TokenMizer provides a queryable alternative to text-retention baselines at half the token cost.
2606.06337.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）在長期任務中的部署面臨一個基本限制：上下文窗口是有限的，而生產性工作會話則不是。當歷史超過最大有效上下文窗口（MECW）時，關鍵的結構化信息——架構決策、任務轉換、文件歷史——會被靜默丟棄。現有的緩解措施將歷史視為平面文本，破壞了使會話可恢復的關係結構。我們提出了TokenMizer，一個開源代理系統，將LLM會話歷史建模為類型化知識圖譜。該架構定義了14種節點類型和7種邊緣類型。一個混合提取管道逐步填充圖譜，而一個三層檢查點系統將其序列化為緊湊的恢復區塊。一個8層壓縮管道減少了上下文開銷，而語義緩存減少了重複查詢的延遲。在一個涵蓋5個領域的21個會話的受控基準上進行評估，TokenMizer顯示出顯著的令牌經濟。它產生的恢復區塊平均為78個令牌（範圍：42-124）——比評估基準（159-170個令牌）小2倍——同時實現了更高的決策召回率（+9-17個百分點）。至關重要的是，基準僅保留技術被提及的事實；TokenMizer保留了理由。在所有會話中，TokenMizer實現了平均任務召回率51.0%、決策召回率46.6%和文件召回率58.7%。方差反映了領域的異質性：明確的命令性措辭（軟體工程）得分高於隱含推理（研究）。消融研究顯示模糊標籤匹配是主要的改進因素（+33個百分點的任務召回率）。啟發式壓縮實現了47.3%的令牌減少，且沒有外部依賴。TokenMizer提供了一個可查詢的替代方案，以半數的令牌成本取代文本保留基準。
2606.06337.author: Shweta Mishra et.al.
